name: New Hampshire SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income New Hampshire households through the DHHS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL under broad-based categorical eligibility

name: New Hampshire Medicaid
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income New Hampshire residents through managed care plans including Granite Advantage for adults.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 318% FPL; pregnant women up to 196% FPL

name: New Hampshire Employment Security - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to New Hampshire workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: New Hampshire Housing Finance Authority
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgages, rental assistance, Housing Choice Vouchers, homelessness prevention, and housing counseling statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: New Hampshire Legal Assistance
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income New Hampshire residents in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and disability rights.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income New Hampshire residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: NH Works - Workforce Development Centers
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, resume workshops, career counseling, and training referrals at locations statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Any New Hampshire resident seeking employment or career development services

name: New Hampshire Fuel Assistance Program (FAP)
description: LIHEAP-funded program helping eligible low-income New Hampshire households pay heating costs during winter through fuel assistance grants.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: 211 New Hampshire
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting New Hampshire residents with health and human services resources around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any New Hampshire resident seeking help or information about available services

name: New Hampshire Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate and the state's only food bank distributing food to over 400 partner agencies including pantries, shelters, and meal programs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across New Hampshire

name: New Hampshire State Office of Veterans Services
description: Assists New Hampshire veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, and connecting to state and federal VA services.
eligibilitySummary: New Hampshire veterans and their families

name: NH Rapid Response Access Point - Mental Health Crisis
description: Statewide mental health crisis line providing immediate support, crisis intervention, mobile crisis teams, and referrals to mental health services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in New Hampshire experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: New Hampshire Child Care Scholarship Program
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working New Hampshire families afford quality childcare for their children.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% of state median income

name: New Hampshire WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition counseling, and health referrals at clinics statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: New Hampshire TANF / NHEP
description: New Hampshire Employment Program providing temporary financial assistance and employment support services to very low-income families with children.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: Manchester Transit Authority (MTA)
description: Public bus system serving the Manchester area, New Hampshire's largest city, with fixed routes and paratransit services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Manchester area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: COAST - Cooperative Alliance for Seacoast Transportation
description: Public transit serving the New Hampshire Seacoast region including Portsmouth, Dover, Rochester, and surrounding communities.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Seacoast region; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: New Hampshire Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income New Hampshire households to reduce heating costs and improve comfort.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: New Hampshire Adult Education
description: Free adult education programs including GED/HiSET preparation, English language classes, and college and career readiness offered at centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: New Hampshire Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic and sexual violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence or sexual assault in New Hampshire

name: Manchester Housing and Redevelopment Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in Manchester, New Hampshire's largest city.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Manchester area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: New Hampshire Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for New Hampshire residents with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: New Hampshire residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: New Hampshire CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides health coverage for uninsured children in New Hampshire families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 318% of federal poverty level

name: New Hampshire Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Federal health insurance marketplace where New Hampshire residents can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: New Hampshire residents without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL

name: New Hampshire Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income New Hampshire residents to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Granite State Community College System - Workforce Training
description: Workforce development and career training programs through New Hampshire's community college system including certificates, apprenticeships, and customized training.
eligibilitySummary: Adults seeking career training and workforce education
incomeLimitNotes: Financial aid available; Granite Guarantee covers tuition for Pell-eligible students

name: ServiceLink - Aging and Disability Resource Center
description: Network of resource centers across New Hampshire connecting seniors and people with disabilities to long-term services, Medicare counseling, and benefits assistance.
eligibilitySummary: New Hampshire residents age 60+, individuals with disabilities, and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: 603 Legal Aid (New Hampshire Pro Bono)
description: Pro bono legal referral program matching low-income New Hampshire residents with volunteer attorneys for civil legal matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income New Hampshire residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Nashua Transit System
description: Public bus system serving Nashua, New Hampshire's second-largest city, with fixed routes and demand-response transit options.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Nashua area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: Southern New Hampshire Services - Community Action
description: Community action agency serving southern New Hampshire with fuel assistance, weatherization, emergency services, Head Start, and housing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of southern New Hampshire
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Tri-County Community Action - Northern NH
description: Community action agency serving northern New Hampshire's Coos, Carroll, and Grafton counties with fuel assistance, housing, food programs, and emergency services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of northern New Hampshire
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: New Hampshire Housing Stability Program
description: Emergency rental assistance program helping New Hampshire tenants at risk of eviction maintain stable housing through rent and utility assistance.
eligibilitySummary: New Hampshire tenants at risk of eviction or housing instability
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% area median income
